session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9057 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll start by loading the skill and examining its instructions, then execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [195 lines omitted] ...
131→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 133→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 171→ "error": null 185→## Error Case 199→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 217→ "error": null

session.assistant:
Perfect. Now I'll classify all the PR feedback according to the Comment Classification Model. Let me analyze:
**PR Data:** - PR Number: 9057 - Title: "Move consolidate-learn-plans-plan command to system folder and document system folder convention" - URL: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9057
**Review Threads:** None
**Discussion Comments Analysis:** 1. **Comment 4023758917** (schrockn) - "Plan Queued for Implementation" - Status update, informational 2. **Comment 4023761417** (github-actions[bot]) - "GitHub Action Started" - CI status update, informational 3. **Comment 4023816449** (github-actions[bot]) - "Dignified Python Review" - Bot review suggestion, informational (0 violations, all clear) 4. **Comment 4023817550** (github-actions[bot]) - "Dignified Code Simplifier Review" - Bot review suggestion, informational (0 violations) 5. **Comment 4023817794** (github-actions[bot]) - "Test Coverage Review" - Bot review suggestion, informational (0 violations) 6. **Comment 4023822280** (github-actions[bot]) - "Tripwires Review" - Bot review suggestion, informational (0 violations) 7. **Comment 4023826806** (github-actions[bot]) - "Audit PR Docs" - **ACTIONABLE** - Found 1 violation: drift-risk section in system-folder-convention.md line 25
**File Restructuring Context:** - File move detected: `.../{ => system}/consolidate-learn-plans-plan.md` (file renamed/moved) - Documentation files added/modified - No pre-existing code issues detected
**Classification:**
The only actionable item is the Audit PR Docs comment about the drift-risk violation in the documentation. This is a human-relevant actionable item (documentation improvement) even though it came from a bot. All other comments are informational CI status updates and clean review results.
However, looking at the structure more carefully: the "Audit PR Docs" comment is about a documentation issue that needs fixing - this is actionable content. It's a single file violation at a specific line, making it a `local` complexity item.
```json { "success": true, "pr_number": 9057, "pr_title": "Move consolidate-learn-plans-plan command to system folder and document system folder convention", "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9057", "actionable_threads": [ { "thread_id": "discussion-4023826806", "type": "discussion", "path": "docs/learned/commands/system-folder-convention.md", "line": 25, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Remove or simplify 'Current System Commands' table to avoid drift risk", "complexity": "local", "original_comment": "Drift-risk sections — Found in docs/learned/commands/system-folder-convention.md:25. \"Current System Commands\" table enumerates specific filenames — will go stale as system commands are added/removed" } ], "discussion_actions": [ { "comment_id": 4023826806, "action_summary": "Remove or simplify 'Current System Commands' table to avoid drift risk", "complexity": "local", "original_comment": "Drift-risk sections — Found in docs/learned/commands/system-folder-convention.md:25. \"Current System Commands\" table enumerates specific filenames — will go stale as system commands are added/removed" } ], "informational_count": 6, "batches": [ { "name": "Pre-Existing (Auto-Resolve)", "complexity": "pre_existing", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Local Fixes", "complexity": "local", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [0] }, { "name": "Single-File", "complexity": "single_file", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Cross-Cutting", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Complex", "complexity": "complex", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Informational", "complexity": "informational", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] } ], "error": null } ```
